matchReport.analyst: CPI morning. TSLA gapped down hard and spent the entire session attacking one major support shelf - six hours of pressure, heavy absorption by passive buyers, and the level never broke cleanly. Theo identified the conditions as chop-with-trapdoor-risk: shorts into absorption get squeezed, longs against a falling tape get run over. Verdict: no edge, no trade. The new confirmation discipline vetoed two tempting fakeout entries; in the replay both setups failed to confirm and reversed. A zero-trade day that likely protected meaningful simulated risk is a win for the process, even though the scoreboard shows 0.0R.
matchReport.commentator: What a siege! The away side hammered the home goal for SIX HOURS - wave after wave at the same defensive line - and the keeper saved everything. Theo's manager kept every striker on the bench: 'that pitch was a trap,' he said after the whistle. Two players warmed up twice and were pulled back both times - and on the replay, neither run looked like it ends well. Full time: 0-0. Sometimes the best result is the match you refuse to play.
